sections.paragraph:
f. (from the root חָמַם)-

(1) heat, of the sun, Psa 19:7.

(2) poetically for the sun itself, Job 30:28 Son 6:10 Isa 30:26. (So often in the Mishnah.)

I. חֵמָה f. constr. חֲמַת (for יְחֵמָה from the root יָחַם),-

(1) warmth [“sc. from wine, Hos 7:5 ”], anger (Arab. حِمْيَةُ, حُمَّةُ), Gen 27:44 Jer 6:11. הַחֵמָה כֹּוס הַיַּיִן Jer 25:15, and כֹּוס הַחֵמָה Isa 51:17, the cup of wrath of which Jehovah makes the nations drink, comp. Rev 16:19 and Job 21:20, “let him drink of the wrath of the Almighty.”

(2) poison (as that which burns the bowels), Deu 32:24 Psa 58:5. Arab. حُمَةُ poison of a scorpion. Eth. ሐምት፡ poison.

II. חֵמָה i.q. חֶמְאָה (with the radical א omitted), f. milk, Job 29:6.
